title: Semi-Quantitative Characterization of NH₄⁺ and K⁺-Mediated Inhibition of Pyrobaculum calidifontis DNA Polymerase Using Endpoint PCR
author: Laiq, Muhammad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study provides a semi-quantitative analysis of monovalent cation-mediated inhibition of recombinant  Pyrobaculum calidifontis  (Pca) DNA polymerase. Using endpoint PCR and densitometric analysis, the research determines that K +  is a significantly more potent inhibitor (IC 50  = 4.74 mM) compared to NH 4  +  (IC 50  = 32.90 mM). A critical inhibitory threshold was identified in the 20-30 mM range for both ions. These findings offer a framework for optimizing PCR buffer conditions for high-fidelity archaeal polymerases.
steps_raw: This study provides a semi-quantitative analysis of monovalent cation-mediated inhibition of recombinant  Pyrobaculum calidifontis  (Pca) DNA polymerase. A critical inhibitory threshold was identified in the 20-30 mM range for both ions. These findings offer a framework for optimizing PCR buffer conditions for high-fidelity archaeal polymerases.
